extractedEntities.name: Code-First Ontology Development
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where business logic and data transformations are authored in code with full IDE support, version control, and code review workflows.

keyInsights: Code Repositories supports three repository types: Transforms (Python/Java/SQL data transformations), Functions (TypeScript/Python business logic), and Model development.
Functions Repositories provide native Ontology data access with autocomplete based on Ontology data types, enabling type-safe business logic development.
All common Git workflows (branching, committing, tagging, pull requests) are supported through the web UI with configurable permissions.
Functions can be written in TypeScript or Python and execute with low latency in operational contexts with full Ontology access.
VS Code with the Palantir extension provides an alternative local IDE experience to the web-based Code Repositories.

title: Overview and Getting Started - Breaking Changes Between OSv1 and OSv2 - Palantir
summary: Documents the breaking changes between Object Storage V1 (Phonograph) and V2, including the rename of writeback datasets to materializations, materializations becoming optional, and new incremental indexing capabilities. Critical migration reference for understanding Ontology data storage evolution.
relevantParts: OSv1 to OSv2 migration breaking changes
Architectural evolution of the Ontology backend

extractedEntities.name: Object Storage V1
extractedEntities.description: Legacy object storage system (Phonograph) with a large API surface area exposing low-level database functionality directly. Being replaced by OSv2.

extractedEntities.name: Object Storage V2
extractedEntities.description: New object storage architecture that syncs objects through the Object Data Funnel service into specialized object databases for improved scale, performance, flexibility, and security.

extractedEntities.name: Object Data Funnel
extractedEntities.description: OSv2 service that syncs objects into specialized object databases, replacing OSv1's monolithic database approach.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity. OSv2 enforces stricter data restrictions on primary keys and properties compared to OSv1.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Attribute of an Object Type. OSv2 enforces data restrictions on property values that were not enforced in OSv1.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: OSv2 requires all user edits to go through Actions, replacing direct OSv1 edit API calls.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Defines relationships between Object Types. OSv1 required writeback datasets for many-to-many link types with join tables.

extractedEntities.name: Materialization
extractedEntities.description: OSv2 term for what was called 'writeback dataset' in OSv1. Optional in OSv2 whereas writeback datasets were required in OSv1.

extractedEntities.name: Action Log
extractedEntities.description: Object type that captures how Actions affect objects, providing an alternative to full edit history tracking in OSv2.

extractedEntities.name: OSv1 to OSv2 Migration
extractedEntities.description: Migration process from Phonograph to OSv2 requiring addressing permanent breaking changes including Actions-only edits, data restrictions, and optional materializations.

extractedEntities.name: Edit History Preservation
extractedEntities.description: Optional OSv2 migration feature to include full edit history from OSv1, incurring additional compute and storage costs.

extractedEntities.name: Specialized Object Databases
extractedEntities.description: OSv2 architecture pattern where different specialized databases handle specific use cases, replacing OSv1's monolithic approach.

keyInsights: OSv2 is a fundamental architectural shift from OSv1's monolithic database to specialized object databases synced via the Object Data Funnel service.
All user edits in OSv2 must go through Actions; direct OSv1 edit API calls must be refactored before migration.
Writeback datasets are renamed to materializations in OSv2 and become optional, whereas they were required in OSv1.
Edit history preservation during migration is optional and incurs additional compute costs; without it, full OSv1 edit history is permanently lost.
OSv2 enforces stricter data restrictions on primary keys and properties that were not present in OSv1, requiring data quality remediation before migration.
Action Logs provide an alternative mechanism to track how Actions affect objects, partially replacing the edit history capability.
The migration involves two categories of breaking changes: specialized databases lacking some OSv1 functionality, and refactoring of consolidated OSv1 queries.

title: Iceberg Tables - Managed vs Virtual Iceberg Tables - Palantir
summary: Compares managed Iceberg tables (managed by Foundry's Iceberg catalog) with virtual Iceberg tables (using external storage and catalogs like Unity, Glue, or Polaris). Covers tradeoffs between data locality and external system integration.
relevantParts: Managed vs virtual Iceberg table architecture comparison

extractedEntities.name: Managed Iceberg Table
extractedEntities.description: Iceberg table managed by Foundry's Iceberg catalog, supporting additional functionality like guided frontends for maintenance operations.

extractedEntities.name: Virtual Iceberg Table
extractedEntities.description: Virtual table using external storage and an external Iceberg catalog (Unity, Glue, or Polaris) without data copying between external and Foundry storage.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry Iceberg Catalog
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's implementation of the Iceberg REST catalog that manages Iceberg tables and enables guided frontend configurations.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Platform supporting both managed and virtual Iceberg tables natively with most core features.

extractedEntities.name: Bring-Your-Own-Bucket Storage
extractedEntities.description: Storage option for managed Iceberg tables where customers use their own AWS or Azure storage buckets. In Beta.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry Managed Storage
extractedEntities.description: Storage option where Foundry manages the storage for Iceberg tables. In Beta, AWS only.

extractedEntities.name: External Iceberg Catalog
extractedEntities.description: Third-party Iceberg catalog integration (Unity, Glue, Polaris) used for virtual Iceberg tables.

extractedEntities.name: Zero-Copy Data Integration
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where virtual tables reference external data in place without copying it into Foundry storage, avoiding data duplication.

keyInsights: Foundry supports two Iceberg table types: managed (Foundry's catalog) and virtual (external catalog like Unity, Glue, or Polaris).
Virtual Iceberg tables enable zero-copy integration with no data duplication between external storage and Foundry.
Managed Iceberg tables offer additional Foundry functionality like guided frontends for maintenance operations through the Iceberg REST catalog.
Three storage configurations exist: bring-your-own-bucket (AWS/Azure), Foundry-managed storage (AWS only), and external virtual tables, all currently in Beta.
Both managed and virtual Iceberg tables work natively with most core Foundry features.

summary: Documents the source exploration feature in Data Connection that allows users to browse and discover available data within connected external systems before setting up syncs.

extractedEntities.name: Data Connection

extractedEntities.name: Data Discovery Before Sync
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of exploring external source data (tables, views, files) via the Explore page before configuring syncs, allowing users to preview data and understand relationships.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform that receives data from external sources via Data Connection syncs.

keyInsights: Data Connection's source exploration provides two modes: a graph interface for table-based data (showing relationships via foreign keys) and a directory browser for file-based data.
The table exploration graph automatically displays relationships between tables by highlighting foreign keys when selecting related tables.
Syncs can be created directly from the exploration graph by right-clicking on a table, streamlining the discovery-to-ingestion workflow.
File-based source exploration supports filtering to control which files get imported, allowing selective ingestion from directories.
The graph view is not always available for table-based sources — for example, REST API models may lack clear relations between objects.

title: Palantir Foundry's dataset version control, a diff-based Git for data - Hacker News
summary: Hacker News discussion about Foundry's dataset version control system, comparing it to a diff-based Git for data. Community discussion of the technical approach and its tradeoffs versus other data versioning systems.
relevantParts: Community analysis of Foundry's Git-like data versioning
Comparison with other data versioning approaches

extractedEntities.name: Dataset Version Control
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's diff-based version control system for datasets, storing changes as separate diffs in the backing filesystem. Particularly useful for append-only immutable datasets like system logs or sensor readings.

extractedEntities.name: Dataset
extractedEntities.description: Foundry's core data abstraction with built-in version control. Each diff is stored in a separate folder (e.g., datasetA/diff1, datasetA/diff2) in the backing filesystem.

extractedEntities.name: Dataset Diff Storage
extractedEntities.description: Implementation detail where each dataset diff is stored in a separate folder in the backing filesystem. The whole dataset is represented by the union of all diff folders (datasetA/*).

extractedEntities.name: ACID Guarantees
extractedEntities.description: Transactional guarantees provided by Foundry's dataset version control system, tied to the versioning and sandboxing features of the platform.

extractedEntities.name: Diff-Based Data Versioning
extractedEntities.description: Architectural pattern where dataset changes are stored as incremental diffs rather than full snapshots, analogous to Git but for tabular data.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform that includes a built-in dataset version control system with diff-based storage, versioning, and sandboxing features.

keyInsights: Foundry's dataset version control stores diffs in separate folders in the backing filesystem (datasetA/diff1, datasetA/diff2), with the full dataset represented by their union.
The diff-based approach is particularly optimized for append-only immutable datasets like system logs and sensor readings, which are among the largest and fastest-growing datasets.
Delete operations in the version control are handled logically rather than physically — the stored diffs may not reflect the actual current dataset state.
Exported datasets from Foundry lose their versioning and sandboxing features, creating a form of platform lock-in tied to ACID guarantees.
The versioning system provides sandboxing capabilities in addition to version tracking, enabling isolated data experimentation.

extractedEntities.name: Lineage Exploration

extractedEntities.name: Code Repositories
extractedEntities.description: Foundry tool whose original code can be viewed and modified from Data Lineage.

keyInsights: Data Lineage supports both datasets and object types as resources in the lineage graph, bridging the raw data and Ontology layers.
The graph supports multi-level expansion with configurable depth, including full expansion to raw data sources or final descendants.
Performance is a consideration — adding too many nodes simultaneously can degrade graph performance, so node counts should be managed.
Data Lineage integrates with Code Workbook and Code Repositories, allowing users to view the transformation code that created any dataset directly from the lineage graph.
The 'find relation between nodes' feature can discover all intermediate nodes or common ancestors/descendants between two selected resources.

Governance in Foundry is embedded in the platform design rather than bolted on, with policies inheriting through project structure and data lineage.

title: Core Concepts - Views - Palantir
summary: Documents views in Foundry's data integration layer, which provide derived or filtered perspectives on underlying datasets without duplicating data, supporting data projection and access patterns.

extractedEntities.name: View
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry resource that behaves like a dataset but holds no data files. Composed of the union of backing datasets when read, supporting automatic updates, folder organization, and primary key deduplication.

extractedEntities.name: Dataset
extractedEntities.description: A core Foundry resource that stores structured data in files. Views can use datasets as backing sources and can be used as transform inputs but not outputs.

extractedEntities.name: Backing Dataset
extractedEntities.description: One or more datasets that compose a View through union. All backing datasets for a given View must have the same column names and types.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core data platform supporting data integration, transformation, governance, and operational applications.

extractedEntities.name: View Primary Key
extractedEntities.description: A configuration on Views that enables automatic deduplication by guaranteeing that each primary key appears in only one row. Once set, the primary key cannot be modified without creating a new View.

extractedEntities.name: View Deduplication Order
extractedEntities.description: Configuration specifying which row to retain when duplicate primary keys exist. Options include selecting by backing dataset priority order or by a deduplication column (latest timestamp, highest value, etc.).

extractedEntities.name: Zero-Copy Dataset Replication
extractedEntities.description: Using Views to replicate a dataset in a different folder location without incurring additional compute and storage costs.

extractedEntities.name: Automatic Union Updates
extractedEntities.description: A View pattern where a build schedule automatically rebuilds the View whenever backing datasets update, providing always-up-to-date union results without manual transforms.

keyInsights: Views are virtual datasets that hold no data files — they are composed by unioning backing datasets at read time, enabling near-instant builds.
Primary key deduplication on Views guarantees uniqueness without transforms, but the key is immutable once set and requires creating a new View to change.
Views can serve as transform inputs but cannot be transform outputs, and should not be used as incremental inputs since deduplication operates within the transaction range only.
All backing datasets must share the same column names and types (though column order can differ), enforcing schema consistency across unioned sources.
Views enable zero-copy dataset replication across different folder locations without additional compute or storage costs.

extractedEntities.name: Rule Logic JSON Grammar
extractedEntities.description: Match conditions for rules stored as a compressed JSON blob that conforms to a specific grammar for consistent serialization.

keyInsights: Foundry Rules models both Rules and Proposals as standard Ontology objects, demonstrating how the Ontology serves as a universal modeling layer even for system-level constructs.
Rule logic is stored as a compressed JSON blob conforming to a specific grammar, enabling consistent serialization and programmatic manipulation of match conditions.
The Proposal workflow is analogous to software pull requests, supporting multiple concurrent proposals per rule with full audit trail (author, timestamp, status, reviewer).
Proposals are optional — since Foundry Rules uses standard objects and Actions, the approval workflow can be customized to match any operational or regulatory requirements.
Rules support both alerting patterns (severity, assignee, priority) and categorization patterns (group, sub-group), with custom properties configurable per workflow.

title: Overview - AIP - Palantir Docs

Security and governance model for AI operations

keyInsights: AIP provides a complete AI development lifecycle: governed LLM access, function building (AIP Logic), agent creation (Agent Studio), evaluation (Evals), and observability — all integrated with the Ontology.
AIP is designed for everyone in an organization, from developers to frontline users, indicating a democratization approach to AI rather than limiting it to technical teams.
AIP together with Foundry and Apollo forms an operating system capable of delivering AI-driven products ranging from LLM-powered web apps to mobile vision-language apps to edge-embedded AI.
AI governance is built into AIP's core with security measures, access controls, encryption, auditing, and historical lineage tracking for AI operations.
AIP's agent capabilities allow LLMs to execute Actions in the Ontology, creating a closed loop between AI reasoning and operational systems rather than just generating text outputs.

title: Semantic search - Ontology augmented generation - Palantir Docs
summary: Official documentation on Ontology Augmented Generation (OAG). Explains how OAG extends RAG by grounding LLMs in the Ontology's decision-centric model that combines data, logic, and actions. Covers semantic search implementation for querying Ontology objects from LLM contexts.
relevantParts: OAG as an extension of RAG grounded in the Ontology
Semantic search over Ontology objects using vector embeddings
Integration of data, logic, and actions for LLM grounding

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: An extension of RAG that grounds LLMs in the Ontology's decision-centric model combining data, logic, and actions, enabling retrieval of structured enterprise context rather than just document chunks.

extractedEntities.name: Retrieval Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: The foundational pattern of finding and providing relevant context to LLMs, which OAG extends by leveraging the Ontology's structured semantic model.

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: A retrieval approach using embedding-based similarity search over chunked Ontology objects with media reference properties to find relevant context for LLM prompts.

extractedEntities.name: HyDE
extractedEntities.description: Hypothetical Document Embeddings — a retrieval improvement technique where an LLM generates a hypothetical answer chunk before embedding it for similarity search, bridging the asymmetry between queries and answers.

extractedEntities.name: Chunking Strategy
extractedEntities.description: The approach for splitting documents or data into smaller segments (chunks) stored as Ontology objects with media reference properties for semantic search retrieval.

extractedEntities.name: Hybrid Search
extractedEntities.description: A retrieval approach combining semantic (embedding-based) search with keyword-based search to improve recall and precision in OAG workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Query Augmentation
extractedEntities.description: A retrieval technique that modifies or expands the user's original query to improve search results in OAG contexts.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: A core Ontology concept that can represent document chunks with media reference properties for semantic search in OAG workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: An attribute of an Object Type, including media reference properties that link chunk objects to their source documents for semantic search.

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's application builder that provides a PDF Viewer widget for displaying source documents retrieved through semantic search.

keyInsights: OAG extends RAG by leveraging the Ontology's structured semantic model rather than just retrieving document chunks, grounding LLMs in enterprise-specific data, logic, and actions.
With increasing model context lengths (e.g., 128k tokens for GPT-4o), full-context approaches may eliminate the need for semantic search when the corpus fits within the context window.
The recommended implementation path is to start with basic semantic search and incrementally add techniques like HyDE, hybrid search, and query augmentation only as retrieval quality issues emerge.
HyDE improves retrieval by generating a hypothetical answer chunk before embedding, which bridges the semantic gap between user queries and document content formats.
Chunk objects in the Ontology use media reference properties to maintain links back to source documents, enabling traceability from LLM responses to original sources.

title: Building with Palantir AIP: Data Tools for RAG / OAG - Palantir Blog
summary: Palantir engineering blog post explaining how to build data tools that enable LLMs to query the Ontology for trusted data. Covers how equipping LLMs with Ontology query capabilities is a significant step in reducing hallucinations. Details the data tool paradigm within OAG.
relevantParts: How data tools allow LLMs to query Ontology objects
Practical implementation of OAG data retrieval
Hallucination reduction through trusted data access

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: A decision-centric extension of RAG that allows LLMs to leverage not just data retrieval but also deterministic logic tools (forecasts, optimizers) and actions through the Palantir Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Data Tools
extractedEntities.description: Ontology query capabilities given to LLMs in AIP, enabling them to search, filter, and aggregate objects in the Ontology to retrieve trusted enterprise data as context for generation.

extractedEntities.name: HyperAuto
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's Software-Defined Data Integration (SDDI) suite that provides end-to-end data integration from source systems like SAP to the Ontology in minutes, leveraging source metadata for autonomous pipeline creation.

extractedEntities.name: Chain of Thought Reasoning
extractedEntities.description: The visible reasoning steps taken by the LLM in OAG workflows, showing which Ontology objects were accessed and how conclusions were reached, providing transparency and auditability.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type

extractedEntities.name: Link Type

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Deterministic logic tools (forecasts, optimizers) in the Ontology that LLMs can invoke through OAG to go beyond data retrieval into computation.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that hosts OAG capabilities, data tools, and the builder tools for creating AI-powered operational workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Distribution Center Fire Response
extractedEntities.description: A fictional scenario demonstrating OAG where Titan Industries uses AIP to assess the impact of a fire at a distribution center, identify affected orders, and surface solutions for redistributing inventory.

extractedEntities.name: Data Health
extractedEntities.description: An AIP suite of tools for maintaining clean and healthy data flowing from HyperAuto pipelines into the Ontology.

keyInsights: OAG goes beyond RAG by enabling LLMs to not only retrieve data but also invoke deterministic logic tools (forecasts, optimizers) and execute actions through the Ontology, creating a full decision-action loop.
Data tools equip LLMs with Ontology query capabilities, allowing them to search, filter, and aggregate objects programmatically rather than relying solely on pre-embedded document chunks.
HyperAuto (Software-Defined Data Integration) can create end-to-end pipelines from source systems like SAP to Ontology objects in minutes using source metadata for autonomous pipeline generation.
Chain of Thought reasoning in OAG provides transparency by showing exactly which Ontology objects were accessed and how conclusions were reached, building trust through auditability.
The OAG pattern grounds LLMs in the enterprise's operational reality through the Ontology, significantly reducing hallucination risk by anchoring responses in deterministic, verified enterprise data.

title: Building with Palantir AIP: Logic Tools for RAG/OAG - Palantir Blog

relevantParts: Logic tools combining deterministic computation with LLM reasoning
Integration of forecasting models and optimizers as LLM tools
Enterprise-scale AI that leverages existing logic assets

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: A decision-centric extension of RAG that grounds LLMs in an enterprise's operational reality via the Ontology, unifying data, logic, and actions in a single system.

keyInsights: OAG extends RAG by grounding LLMs in the three pillars of enterprise decision-making: data, logic, and actions, all unified through the Ontology.
Logic tools bridge the gap between LLM strengths (contextual reasoning) and traditional computation (forecasting, linear optimization) by letting LLMs invoke deterministic functions.
The ModelOps lifecycle in AIP covers the full span from problem definition through deployment and monitoring, supporting both in-platform and externally-built models.
Enterprise logic assets (forecasting models, optimizers, business rules) can be wrapped as Ontology functions and made available to LLMs without sacrificing trust or determinism.
The supply chain use case demonstrates how OAG logic tools combine historical Ontology data retrieval with external forecasting (Prophet) to support operational recovery decisions.

title: Reducing Hallucinations with the Ontology in Palantir AIP - Palantir Blog
summary: Part 1 of the 'Engineering Responsible AI' series. Explains how AIP prevents hallucinations by giving LLMs the right context from the start via the Ontology. Rather than fixing hallucinations after they occur, OAG retrieves structured objects and their connections, keeping reasoning narrow and accurate.
relevantParts: Structured object retrieval vs. traditional text-based RAG
Ontology metadata augmentation for LLM prompt guidance

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: AIP's approach to hallucination reduction that retrieves structured Ontology objects and their connections to keep LLM reasoning narrow, accurate, and grounded in operational reality.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology element representing structured entities that provide grounded context to LLMs, reducing the risk of hallucination by supplying factual data.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Typed attributes of Object Types that supply specific, structured data points to LLMs as context, constraining responses to factual information.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationship between Object Types that allows LLMs to traverse connected data, enabling richer contextual grounding.

extractedEntities.name: Object View
extractedEntities.description: A curated view of an Object Type that controls which properties and links are visible to LLMs, limiting context to only what is necessary for a task.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that reduces hallucinations by giving LLMs the right structured context from the Ontology from the start, rather than correcting after the fact.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology logic that can be surfaced as a tool for LLMs, enabling them to perform structured operations on grounded data.

extractedEntities.name: Retrieval Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: A foundational technique for providing LLMs with external context, which OAG extends by using structured Ontology objects rather than unstructured documents.

extractedEntities.name: Next Token Prediction
extractedEntities.description: The fundamental LLM mechanism that predicts statistically likely continuations without requiring truthfulness, which is the root cause of hallucinations.

extractedEntities.name: Context Window Grounding
extractedEntities.description: The strategy of focusing on what LLMs are suited for (text generation) while supplying structured, relevant context to constrain outputs to factual information.

keyInsights: Hallucinations stem from the fundamental nature of LLMs as next-token predictors: they generate statistically plausible text without requiring truthfulness.
AIP's approach is preventive rather than corrective -- it gives LLMs the right structured context from the start via the Ontology, rather than trying to fix hallucinations after they occur.
OAG provides structured objects with typed properties and relationships, which is more constraining and reliable than traditional RAG's unstructured document retrieval.
Object Views control which properties and links are visible to LLMs, implementing the principle of least privilege for AI context to minimize hallucination risk.
The key insight is to focus on what LLMs are actually suited for (generating text from context) while ensuring the context itself is grounded in structured, verified Ontology data.

title: How Palantir AIP Improves LLM Explainability - Palantir Blog
summary: Blog post on improving LLM explainability through AIP. Discusses how AIP Logic tools enable delegation of specific tasks to purpose-built, interpretable tools rather than relying solely on LLM processing, creating composable and auditable AI systems. Covers LLM Debugger for tracking orchestration.
relevantParts: LLM explainability through composable, auditable tool chains
AIP Logic Debugger for tracking LLM orchestration and tool handoffs
Transparent AI reasoning via Ontology grounding

extractedEntities.name: Chain-of-Thought Prompting
extractedEntities.description: An LLM prompting strategy that guides models to produce intermediate reasoning steps, improving transparency and enabling users to trace how conclusions are reached.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Tool

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that improves explainability by decomposing AI workflows into transparent, traceable components through tool delegation and orchestration logging.

extractedEntities.name: LLM Debugger

extractedEntities.name: Function

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Structured Ontology entities that provide transparent, verifiable data context for LLM reasoning, contributing to system-level explainability.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type

extractedEntities.name: Tool Delegation
extractedEntities.description: An architectural pattern where LLMs delegate specific tasks to purpose-built, interpretable tools rather than processing everything internally, creating composable and auditable systems.

extractedEntities.name: Orchestration Trace

extractedEntities.name: Explainable AI
extractedEntities.description: A subfield of AI focused on building interpretable models and understanding the cause of predictions, applied at the system level through transparent component composition.

keyInsights: AIP achieves explainability at the system level by decomposing AI workflows into transparent, traceable components rather than trying to explain individual model internals.
Tool delegation is a key pattern: LLMs invoke deterministic, purpose-built tools for specific tasks, creating composable systems where each component can be independently inspected.
The LLM Debugger provides full orchestration traces showing every tool call, input, output, and reasoning step, making the entire AI workflow auditable.
Chain-of-Thought prompting complements tool delegation by making LLM intermediate reasoning visible, but system-level transparency is more important than individual model explainability.
Explainability goes hand-in-hand with transparency -- understanding the AI system's constituent parts and their interactions is more practical than fully understanding any single model.

title: AIP Logic - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for AIP Logic, a no-code development environment for building LLM-powered functions. Functions are composed of blocks (Ontology read/write, calculations, aggregations, LLM interactions) that chain together. Leverages three categories of Ontology-driven tools: data, logic, and action.
relevantParts: No-code environment for building LLM-powered functions
Three tool categories: data, logic, and action
LLM orchestration with Ontology read/write capabilities
Three categories of Ontology-driven tools: data, logic, action

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: A no-code development environment for building, testing, and releasing LLM-powered functions that leverage the Ontology through an intuitive block-based interface.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Function
extractedEntities.description: An LLM-powered function built in AIP Logic that takes inputs (Ontology objects or text strings) and returns outputs (objects, strings) or makes edits to the Ontology.

extractedEntities.name: Logic Block
extractedEntities.description: A composable unit in AIP Logic functions that performs a specific operation: Ontology read/write, calculations, aggregations, or LLM interactions, chained together to form complete functions.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology concept representing reusable logic that can be authored via AIP Logic's no-code interface or through code workspaces.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type

extractedEntities.name: Action Type

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Typed attributes of Object Types that serve as specific data inputs within Logic function blocks.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that AIP Logic is part of, providing the broader framework for AI-powered enterprise workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Human-in-the-Loop Review
extractedEntities.description: A pattern where Logic function Ontology edits can be staged for human review before being applied, ensuring oversight in automated workflows.

keyInsights: AIP Logic democratizes AI function development by providing a no-code block-based interface, removing the complexity of development environments and API calls.
Logic functions have a clear input-output contract: they take Ontology objects or text strings as input and return objects/strings or make Ontology edits.
Ontology edits from Logic functions can be automated with human-in-the-loop review, allowing staged approval before changes are applied.
The block-based composition model chains Ontology reads, writes, calculations, aggregations, and LLM interactions into coherent workflows.
AIP Logic shares the same rigorous security model as the rest of Palantir, including user-level and function-level permissions controlling LLM data access.

Allows building interactive assistants (AIP Agents) equipped with enterprise-specific information and tools powered by LLMs, the Ontology, documents, and custom tools. Agents are deployable internally and externally through the Ontology SDK and platform APIs.

Agents leverage Ontology, documents, and custom tools
Internal and external deployment via OSDK and APIs

Agents can be deployed both internally within the Palantir platform and externally through the OSDK and platform APIs, enabling enterprise-wide and customer-facing AI assistants.
Application variables enable bidirectional state sharing between AIP Agents and Workshop applications, allowing agents to both read and update application context.
The tier framework enables progressive complexity: starting from simple document Q&A to fully autonomous agents that handle complex workflows without human interaction.
AIP Agents are powered by four context sources: LLMs, the Ontology (objects, actions, functions), documents, and custom tools, creating rich enterprise-grounded reasoning.

title: AIP Agent Studio - Tools - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on the tools available in AIP Agent Studio. Includes action tools (ontology edits), object query tools (LLM access to object types), request clarification, commands (interact with Palantir apps on behalf of users), and custom functions. Supports native and prompted tool calling modes.

Native vs prompted tool calling modes for agents
Parallel tool calling support in native mode

extractedEntities.name: Prompted Tool Calling
extractedEntities.description: A tool mode that inserts instructions into the prompt for tool usage, supporting all tool types and models but limited to sequential single-tool calls.

keyInsights: Agent Studio provides six distinct tool types: action, object query, function, update application variable, command, and request clarification, each serving a specific capability.
Object query tools support filtering, aggregation, inspection, and link traversal with configurable property visibility, enabling token-efficient data access for LLMs.
Native tool calling enables parallel tool execution with improved speed and token efficiency, but is limited to specific models and tool types (actions, object query, function, update variable).
Prompted tool calling provides universal compatibility across all models and tool types but is limited to sequential single-tool calls per turn.
Action tools can be configured for automatic execution or user confirmation, providing granular control over the level of human oversight in agentic workflows.
The View Reasoning feature allows inspection of the LLM's decision-making process for each response, supporting transparency and debugging.

extractedEntities.description: A technique in AIP Evals for systematically testing different parameter combinations to evaluate function performance across variations.

keyInsights: AIP Evals is purpose-built for the non-deterministic nature of LLMs, enabling variance examination across multiple runs and cross-version comparison.
Evaluation suites support simultaneous testing of multiple target functions, enabling side-by-side model comparison on identical test cases.
Four evaluator authoring modes are available: no-code, Python, TypeScript, and LLM-as-judge, accommodating different skill levels and evaluation needs.
Metrics are produced per test case and can be compared in aggregate or individually between runs, supporting both macro and micro performance analysis.
The system supports evaluating AIP Logic functions, AIP Agent functions, and code-authored functions within a unified evaluation framework.

title: Semantic search - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation on semantic search in the Ontology. Explains how AI models transform text into vector embeddings for similarity-based retrieval. When embedded text is associated with Ontology objects, search-driven operational workflows become more useful and contextually grounded.
relevantParts: Vector embedding creation from text for semantic search
Association of embeddings with Ontology objects
Implementation approaches: AI Agents, custom functions, document processing

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: A search capability that finds text based on inherent meaning and context using vector embeddings, rather than relying on keywords or traditional search methods.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Embedding
extractedEntities.description: Arrays of numbers produced by AI models that represent text as points in N-dimensional space, where proximity indicates semantic similarity.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that can be associated with embedded text, making search-driven operational workflows contextually grounded in structured data.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Typed attributes of Object Types, including vector properties that store embeddings for semantic search capabilities.

extractedEntities.name: KNN Search
extractedEntities.description: K-nearest-neighbors search that finds the nearest vectors in N-dimensional space to identify semantically related entities.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform that hosts the Ontology and provides the infrastructure for semantic search workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Document Chunking
extractedEntities.description: A technique for splitting documents into smaller pieces before embedding, improving semantic search relevance for long-form content.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Property
extractedEntities.description: A special Property type on Object Types that stores embedding vectors, enabling semantic similarity search against Ontology objects.

keyInsights: Semantic search uses AI models to transform text into vector embeddings where proximity in N-dimensional space indicates semantic similarity, enabling meaning-based retrieval.
Associating embedded text with Ontology objects creates operationally grounded search: finding related entities becomes a matter of finding nearest vectors in embedding space.
Vector properties on Object Types are the bridge between unstructured text and structured Ontology data, enabling hybrid structured-semantic queries.
Palantir supports both platform-provided and custom embedding models, and provides document chunking workflows for improving search quality on long-form content.
Semantic search is a foundational capability for OAG, providing the retrieval mechanism that surfaces relevant Ontology context for LLM reasoning.

title: AIP architecture overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Technical architecture documentation for AIP showing how LLM orchestration, tool calling, and model management are structured. Describes how durable orchestrations can be configured through low-code (AIP Logic) or pro-code (Code Workspaces) interfaces for pipeline-style AI workflows.
relevantParts: AIP technical architecture and orchestration patterns
Durable orchestration via low-code and pro-code interfaces
LLM pipeline and tool calling architecture
Integration of AI with Ontology-driven applications

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform for connecting generative AI into operational domains, structured across 12 capability categories spanning secure LLM integration, context engineering, agents, evaluation, and developer toolchain.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform that operates together with AIP as part of a shared service mesh, providing the data infrastructure and Ontology system.

extractedEntities.name: Apollo
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's deployment and infrastructure management platform that powers AIP and Foundry within the Rubix deployment environment.

extractedEntities.name: Rubix
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's deployment environment within which Apollo deploys and manages AIP and Foundry services.

extractedEntities.name: Durable Orchestration
extractedEntities.description: AIP's execution model for pipeline-style AI workflows that can be configured through low-code (AIP Logic) or pro-code (Code Workspaces) interfaces.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology System
extractedEntities.description: The central system that activates context by integrating data, logic, action, and security into a unified representation of enterprise decision-making, with a language, engine, and toolchain.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry tool for data integration and transformation workflows used in context engineering for AIP.

extractedEntities.name: Code Workspaces
extractedEntities.description: Pro-code development environments in Foundry for building custom functions, models, and orchestrations.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Language
extractedEntities.description: The Ontology's modeling system that represents the nouns and verbs of operational processes into a legible form for both humans and AI agents.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Engine
extractedEntities.description: The execution layer enabling querying of billions of objects, orchestrating tens of thousands of actions, and incorporating feedback-based learning.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology Toolchain
extractedEntities.description: The developer-facing layer empowering builders to create diverse AI-powered applications atop the common Ontology foundation.

extractedEntities.name: Interface
extractedEntities.description: Ontology abstraction referenced in the architecture for enabling polymorphic access across the platform.

extractedEntities.name: Context Engineering
extractedEntities.description: The practice of equipping developers with no-, low-, and pro-code tools for integrating contextual data, logic, and action that power the Ontology and dependent workflows.

keyInsights: The AIP architecture is organized into 12 capability categories: secure LLM integration, observability, context engineering, Ontology system, vector/compute/tool services, security, agents, evaluation, automation, developer toolchain, model management, and deployment.

The Ontology system has three distinct layers: a language (modeling nouns and verbs), an engine (querying billions of objects and orchestrating actions), and a toolchain (developer-facing application building).
AIP and Foundry operate as a shared service mesh powered by Apollo and deployed within Rubix, forming a tightly integrated but conceptually distinct platform stack.

Context engineering spans no-code, low-code, and pro-code tools, supporting all data integration modalities (batch, streaming, real-time CDC) across multiple runtimes (Spark, Flink, DataFusion, Polars).
Durable orchestrations enable pipeline-style AI workflows that can recover from failures and maintain state, configurable through either AIP Logic or Code Workspaces.
The platform is designed to be modular and extensible with respect to models, compute engines, and interfaces, supporting the full range of commercial and open-source LLMs.

title: Palantir MCP - Overview - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for Palantir's Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration. Enables external AI systems to read data and metadata from Foundry using configured user token permissions. Includes Palantir MCP for ontology builders and Ontology MCP for consumers to safely read/write data.
relevantParts: MCP protocol for external AI system integration with Foundry
Two MCP modes: Palantir MCP (builders) and Ontology MCP (consumers)
Tool exposure of object types, action types, and query functions

extractedEntities.name: Palantir MCP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's implementation of the Model Context Protocol that enables AI IDEs and agents to autonomously design, build, edit, and review applications within the Palantir platform.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology MCP
extractedEntities.description: A consumption-focused MCP that enables external AI systems to safely read and write Ontology data through application-scoped object types, action types, and query functions exposed as MCP tools.

extractedEntities.name: OSDK
extractedEntities.description: The Ontology Software Development Kit that Palantir MCP provides context for, including OSDK-specific code examples and repository detection.

extractedEntities.name: Developer Console
extractedEntities.description: A Palantir tool for managing applications and OSDK configurations, which Palantir MCP can search and update.

extractedEntities.name: Model Context Protocol
extractedEntities.description: An open protocol standard for providing AI systems with context and tools, which Palantir implements for platform-wide AI IDE integration.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology entities that both Palantir MCP and Ontology MCP expose to external AI systems for querying and data operations.

extractedEntities.name: Action Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology operations exposed through Ontology MCP as tools that external AI agents can execute within application scope restrictions.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology query functions exposed through Ontology MCP that external systems can invoke for data retrieval.

extractedEntities.name: Copilot Studio
extractedEntities.description: A Microsoft product that can connect to Ontology MCP to execute Palantir actions and write data through external AI agents.

extractedEntities.name: Gemini Enterprise
extractedEntities.description: A Google product that can connect to Ontology MCP for reading and writing Ontology data through external AI systems.

extractedEntities.name: Code Snippet Index
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's searchable index of code examples that MCP uses to provide context for libraries that do not fit specific repository types.

keyInsights: Palantir offers two distinct MCP implementations: Palantir MCP for ontology builders (can modify types but not data) and Ontology MCP for consumers (can safely read/write data but not modify types).
Palantir MCP provides both context (internal library documentation, Foundry architecture knowledge) and tools (ontology exploration, project modification) to external AI agents.
Ontology MCP restricts agent actions through application scopes, ensuring external AI systems can only execute operations within predefined boundaries.
The MCP implementation auto-detects repository type (OSDK, Python transforms, TypeScript functions) and injects tailored context for the detected type.
External AI systems like Copilot Studio and Gemini Enterprise can natively integrate with Palantir's Ontology through the MCP standard, enabling cross-platform AI workflows.
Palantir MCP can autonomously design, build, edit, and review applications covering data integration, ontology configuration, and application development.

title: AIP Agent Studio - Core concepts - Palantir Docs
summary: Core concepts documentation for AIP Agent Studio covering how agents use Ontology, document, or custom function-backed context for targeted interactions. Explains RAG within the agent context, where retrieval-augmented generation dynamically provides LLMs with relevant information from external sources.
relevantParts: Agent context types: Ontology, document, custom function
Dynamic context provision for enterprise-specific interactions

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: Palantir tool for creating interactive AIP Agents equipped with enterprise-specific information and tools, supporting retrieval-augmented generation and function publication.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent
extractedEntities.description: Interactive assistant created in Agent Studio, equipped with enterprise-specific information (Ontology, documents, custom functions) and tools for completing tasks.

extractedEntities.name: Retrieval Context
extractedEntities.description: Information dynamically retrieved in response to each user message from configured data sources, included alongside the user's message to the LLM.

extractedEntities.name: Application State
extractedEntities.description: Application variables within prompts (formerly known as parameters) that customize and control LLM behavior, allowing dynamic input based on task requirements.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology concept enabling agents to be published as Functions for use anywhere in the platform where Functions can be executed.

extractedEntities.name: Tool
extractedEntities.description: External functionality or API that an LLM can use within an agent to perform specific actions or retrieve information beyond its base capabilities.

extractedEntities.name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
extractedEntities.description: RAG pattern that leverages external data sources to dynamically provide LLMs with relevant information, enhancing responses with current and contextually appropriate data.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Embeddings
extractedEntities.description: Numerical representations of text capturing semantic meaning, used to compare and retrieve similar text efficiently for agent retrieval context.

extractedEntities.name: Context Window
extractedEntities.description: The amount of text (tokens) an LLM can process at once, including system prompt, conversation history, retrieval context, application state, and tools.

extractedEntities.name: Agents as Functions
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where agents are published as Functions enabling use in AIP Evals, Automate workflows, and Code Repositories.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: Palantir tool for testing and evaluating LLM-based functions and agents.

extractedEntities.name: Automate
extractedEntities.description: Palantir tool for automating agent workflows using published agent Functions.

keyInsights: AIP Agents combine three types of context: retrieval context (dynamic per-message), application state (configurable variables), and tools (external APIs/functions).
Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) in Agent Studio dynamically fetches relevant content from configured data sources for each user message before sending to the LLM.
Agents can be published as Functions, enabling reuse across the platform in AIP Evals, Automate workflows, and Code Repositories.
Vector embeddings provide the semantic similarity foundation for agent retrieval context, enabling efficient text comparison and retrieval.
The context window includes system prompt, conversation history, retrieval context, application state, and tools; exceeding it forces a new session.
Instructions, tool descriptions, and variable descriptions are compiled into the raw system prompt, teaching the LLM how to complete tasks.

title: Supported LLMs - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation listing all LLMs supported in Palantir AIP. Includes models from OpenAI (GPT-5.2, GPT-4.1, GPT-4o), Anthropic (Claude Opus 4.6, Claude Haiku 4.5, Claude 3.5 Sonnet), Google (Gemini), Meta (Llama), xAI, and others. Covers capacity management via TPM and RPM.
relevantParts: Comprehensive list of supported LLM providers and models
Multi-provider support: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, xAI
LLM capacity management with TPM/RPM metrics

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform that is model-agnostic, supporting a diverse selection of LLMs from multiple providers including xAI, OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, Google, and Mistral.

extractedEntities.name: LLM Model Families
extractedEntities.description: Groupings of related LLM models (e.g., GPT-4 family, Claude family) that enrollment administrators enable or disable through Control Panel.

extractedEntities.name: Model Enablement Interface
extractedEntities.description: Control Panel interface where enrollment administrators manage model states (enabled, disabled, disallowed) and accept legal requirements.

extractedEntities.name: Text Embedding Models
extractedEntities.description: Specialized models for converting text to vector representations, including ada embedding, text-embedding-3-large/small, and Snowflake Arctic Embed.

extractedEntities.name: Georestriction
extractedEntities.description: Security mechanism that limits LLM availability based on geographic restrictions applied to specific enrollments.

extractedEntities.name: Model Legal Acknowledgment
extractedEntities.description: Legal requirement where enrollment administrators must accept provider terms of use before certain models can be enabled.

extractedEntities.name: Tokens Per Minute
extractedEntities.description: TPM - capacity management metric for controlling LLM usage throughput on an enrollment.

extractedEntities.name: Requests Per Minute
extractedEntities.description: RPM - capacity management metric for controlling LLM request rate on an enrollment.

extractedEntities.name: Control Panel
extractedEntities.description: Foundry administration interface where enrollment administrators manage model enablement, capacity, and legal acknowledgments.

keyInsights: AIP is model-agnostic, supporting models from xAI (Grok), OpenAI (GPT), Anthropic (Claude), Meta (Llama), Google (Gemini), and Mistral across multiple model sizes.
LLM availability per enrollment depends on three prerequisites: Palantir integration, legal acknowledgment by administrators, and AIP enablement on the enrollment.
Capacity management uses TPM (tokens per minute) and RPM (requests per minute) metrics to control LLM usage across enrollments.
Text embedding models (ada, text-embedding-3, Snowflake Arctic Embed) are available alongside LLMs for vector-based workflows like semantic search.
Georestriction and legal acknowledgment serve as security gates controlling which models are available on which enrollments.
Model states follow a three-tier system: enabled (available), disabled (awaiting legal acknowledgment), and disallowed (not permitted).

title: AIP features - Palantir Docs

relevantParts: Feature categorization by developer vs. frontline user

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent Studio

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: Foundation for stable, reliable AIP workflows providing systematic testing, debugging, and evaluation of LLM-based functions and prompts.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Threads
extractedEntities.description: Easy-to-use LLM interaction tool requiring no technical setup for document analysis and agent interaction via drag-and-drop.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Assist
extractedEntities.description: LLM-powered support tool providing real-time natural language assistance for navigating the Palantir platform.

extractedEntities.name: Palantir MCP
extractedEntities.description: Tool enabling external AI IDEs and agents to connect to the Palantir platform for Ontology context and Foundry tool access.

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Application builder with AIP-accelerated capabilities for building LLM-powered operational workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: Data pipeline tool with AIP LLM node for embedding AI capabilities into data transformation pipelines.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Ontology concept. LLMs are available in Functions, Transforms, and Jupyter notebooks via Code Workspaces.

extractedEntities.name: LLM-Backed Ontology Workflow
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where LLMs are integrated into Ontology-driven workflows for automation, data processing, and agent-based interactions.

keyInsights: AIP features span six core applications: AIP Logic (no-code functions), Agent Studio (agents), Evals (testing), Threads (ad-hoc analysis), Assist (platform help), and MCP (external IDE integration).
AIP Logic provides a no-code environment for creating AI-powered functions backed by Ontology data, while AIP Agent Studio handles interactive agent creation.
Palantir MCP enables external AI IDEs and agents to connect to the platform, gaining Ontology context and access to Foundry tools.
LLMs are available across the development toolchain including Functions, Transforms, Jupyter notebooks, Pipeline Builder, and Workshop.
AIP Evals is positioned as the foundation for production-ready AI workflows, enabling systematic testing and model comparison.
AIP Threads offers the lowest barrier to entry for LLM interaction with drag-and-drop document analysis requiring no technical setup.

Relationship between AIP and Ontology layers
End-to-end capability from data ingestion to AI-powered operations

extractedEntities.description: Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform providing governed LLM access, LLM-driven functions, agent management, evaluation suites, and automation across enterprise processes.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's data platform upon which AIP is layered, providing the data integration, Ontology, and application layers.

extractedEntities.name: Function

extractedEntities.name: AI Data Governance

keyInsights: AIP sits on top of Foundry as the AI layer, transforming the development toolchain to enable LLMs to power ontology-driven functions and agents.
AIP capabilities include governed LLM access, LLM-driven function building, agent creation and management, evaluation suites, and enterprise process automation.
AIP ensures robust data governance and security while streamlining the deployment, management, and scalability of LLMs.
AIP feature availability is subject to change and may differ between customers, indicating an evolving platform.

title: A Palantir Manual for Evaluating Generative AI - Palantir Blog
summary: Palantir's field manual for evaluating generative AI systems. Provides practical guidance on how to systematically assess and improve LLM-powered workflows. Covers evaluation methodology, metrics selection, and operational best practices for responsible AI deployment.
relevantParts: Systematic evaluation methodology for generative AI
Practical guidance on metrics and assessment

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: Palantir tool for testing generative AI systems, enabling organizations to empirically validate improvements and confidently deploy AI solutions in production.

extractedEntities.name: AI Testing and Evaluation
extractedEntities.description: Systematic methodology for testing generative AI covering ground-truth evaluation data, LLM-as-a-Judge evaluators, perturbation testing, and continuous improvement workflows.

extractedEntities.name: LLM-as-a-Judge
extractedEntities.description: Advanced evaluation technique where an LLM evaluates the outputs of another LLM, useful for assessing generative AI quality at scale.

extractedEntities.name: Perturbation Testing
extractedEntities.description: Advanced evaluation technique that tests AI system robustness by introducing controlled variations in inputs to measure output stability.

extractedEntities.name: End-to-End Workflow Evaluation
extractedEntities.description: Macro-level evaluation approach assessing entire operational workflows where AI is embedded, focusing on business outcomes like throughput or delay reduction.

extractedEntities.name: Testable AI Task Decomposition
extractedEntities.description: Micro-level evaluation approach breaking complex multi-step workflows into smaller components (e.g., document retrieval vs. question answering) for precise evaluation.

extractedEntities.name: Document Retrieval Evaluation
extractedEntities.description: Evaluating the retrieval component of RAG systems separately from the question-answering component for more precise AI assessment.

extractedEntities.name: Ground Truth Evaluation Data
extractedEntities.description: Reference datasets with known correct answers used as the baseline for evaluating generative AI system outputs.

keyInsights: Effective AI T&E requires decomposing complex workflows into testable components (macro end-to-end and micro task-level) rather than treating AI as monolithic chat.
LLM-as-a-Judge and perturbation testing are advanced evaluation techniques that complement basic ground-truth evaluation for production AI systems.

The common pitfall of framing LLM usage as 'chat' is counterproductive for evaluation; instead, specific workflows like summarization, translation, and information retrieval must be tested independently.
Ground-truth evaluation data remains a fundamental building block even as more advanced techniques like LLM-as-a-Judge become available.
AI T&E shares significant commonality with continuous improvement workflows in modern software engineering (debuggers, version control, IDEs).

title: Palantir AIP | Testing & Evaluating AI Systems - Palantir Blog
summary: Part 3 of the 'Engineering Responsible AI' series. Covers the journey from AI prototype to production, focusing on systematic testing and evaluation. Discusses how AIP provides tools for generating audit trails, explanations, and model decision evaluations.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Evals
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's tool for rigorous testing and evaluation of generative AI systems, handling non-determinism, debugging specific inputs, and iterating on prompts and model parameters.

extractedEntities.name: AIP
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's AI Platform providing tools for generating audit trails, explanations, and model decision evaluations.

extractedEntities.name: Prototype to Production AI
extractedEntities.description: Methodology for transitioning AI prototypes to reliable production systems through rigorous testing, evaluation, and iterative improvement.

extractedEntities.name: Responsible AI Engineering
extractedEntities.description: Engineering discipline focused on building safe, reliable, and trustworthy AI systems through systematic T&E and governance.

extractedEntities.name: Prompt Engineering Iteration
extractedEntities.description: Iterative workflow where specific test case failures are used to improve AI systems through prompt engineering, data augmentation, and tool handoffs.

extractedEntities.name: AI Audit Trail
extractedEntities.description: Capability for generating audit trails of AI decisions, supporting governance and accountability in production AI systems.

extractedEntities.name: Inventory Entity Resolution
extractedEntities.description: Real-world operational use case where generative AI resolves inventory entity matching in mission-critical settings.

extractedEntities.name: Infection Risk Alerting

keyInsights: Rigorous T&E is positioned as essential for transitioning AI prototypes to production, not merely a compliance checkbox.
Generative AI T&E differs from traditional ML T&E by enabling prompt engineering iteration on specific failures rather than only aggregate metric evaluation.
AIP Evals handles the unique challenges of generative AI: non-determinism, input-specific debugging, and efficient prompt/parameter iteration.
Building organizational trust in AI systems through robust T&E is a prerequisite for scaling AI use across the enterprise.
Real-world mission-critical use cases like inventory entity resolution and infection risk alerting demonstrate the practical value of Palantir's AI T&E approach.

title: Semantic search - Use Palantir-provided models - Palantir Docs
summary: Step-by-step documentation for creating semantic search workflows using Palantir-provided embedding models. Covers the end-to-end process from generating embeddings to performing KNN searches over object types, with the goal of enabling user input to vector conversion and similarity retrieval.
relevantParts: KNN search implementation over Ontology object types

extractedEntities.name: Vector Property
extractedEntities.description: Object Type property that stores vector embeddings, enabling KNN-based semantic search over objects.

extractedEntities.name: KNN Object Set
extractedEntities.description: Object set type that performs K-nearest-neighbors search over objects with embedding properties, returning the top K semantically similar objects.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity that can be configured with vector type properties for semantic search workflows.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Custom business logic that can implement semantic search with ordered results and additional filtering beyond the no-code KNN object set.

extractedEntities.name: Pipeline Builder
extractedEntities.description: Foundry tool used to embed text as vectors using the Text to Embeddings expression with Palantir-provided models.

extractedEntities.name: Workshop
extractedEntities.description: Application builder where semantic search workflows can be configured using KNN object sets, AIP Agent widgets, or custom function-backed search.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Logic
extractedEntities.description: Tool where custom semantic search functions can be used alongside Workshop.

extractedEntities.name: AIP Agent Studio
extractedEntities.description: Tool for enabling AIP Agents to perform semantic search across objects via Ontology context or semantic search tools.

extractedEntities.name: Text to Embeddings Expression
extractedEntities.description: Pipeline Builder expression that converts text strings to vector embeddings using Palantir-provided models like text-embedding-ada-002.

extractedEntities.name: KNN Search Configuration
extractedEntities.description: Configuration for K-nearest-neighbors search including K-value (1-100), query string variable, and embedding property selection.

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search Workflow
extractedEntities.description: End-to-end pattern from generating embeddings via Pipeline Builder, storing as vector properties, to performing KNN searches in Workshop or via Functions.

keyInsights: Semantic search in Foundry follows a three-step pattern: generate embeddings in Pipeline Builder, store as vector properties on an object type, then search via KNN object sets or custom Functions.
Three approaches exist for semantic search: no-code KNN object set in Workshop, no-code AIP Agent with Ontology search tool, and code-based custom Functions for advanced control.
The KNN object set has a limitation: it cannot sort results by relevancy. Custom Functions are needed for ordered results.
Pipeline Builder's Text to Embeddings expression converts strings to vectors using models like text-embedding-ada-002, making embedding generation a no-code operation.
KNN search supports K-values between 1-100, configurable via Workshop string selector widgets for interactive search experiences.
AIP Agents can be configured with Ontology semantic search tools for natural language-driven semantic search without code.

title: Semantic search - Use custom models - Palantir Docs
summary: Documentation for creating semantic search workflows using custom embedding models instead of Palantir-provided ones. Covers how to bring your own embedding model and integrate it with the Ontology's semantic search infrastructure for specialized domain-specific search.
relevantParts: Custom embedding model integration for semantic search
BYOM (Bring Your Own Model) for embeddings

extractedEntities.name: Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: Ontology capability that enables natural language queries against Object Types by comparing vector embeddings of query text with pre-computed embeddings stored as vector properties on objects.

extractedEntities.name: Vector Property
extractedEntities.description: A special property type on Object Types that stores float array embeddings generated by models, enabling KNN-based semantic similarity search across objects.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity that represents a real-world concept (e.g., Document) backed by a dataset, with properties including vector embeddings for semantic search.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: A named attribute on an Object Type, such as Document_Content or Link, that stores data values including text and vector embeddings.

extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Executable code registered in the Ontology that can be used to query objects via semantic search using KNN operations on vector properties.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's core data platform that hosts modeling objectives, data transforms, and the Ontology where vector-enabled Object Types are created and queried.

extractedEntities.name: KNN Functions on Objects
extractedEntities.description: K-Nearest Neighbors search capability within Foundry that finds the closest objects by comparing vector embeddings, used as the underlying mechanism for semantic search.

extractedEntities.name: Embedding Transform
extractedEntities.description: A data pipeline transform that runs input data through an embedding model (e.g., all-MiniLM-L6-v2) and casts output double arrays to float arrays for storage as vector properties.

extractedEntities.name: Live Modeling Deployment
extractedEntities.description: A runtime deployment of a model in Foundry that enables real-time embedding generation for queries against the semantic search index.

extractedEntities.name: BYOM Semantic Search
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of bringing your own embedding model to Foundry, running transforms to generate embeddings, storing them as vector properties, and querying via KNN Functions.

keyInsights: Semantic search in Foundry works by storing vector embeddings as a special vector property type on Object Types, then using KNN Functions to find nearest neighbors at query time.
Custom embedding models must output float arrays compatible with the Foundry Ontology vector type; double arrays from models need to be cast to floats in the embedding transform.
The BYOM workflow is no longer recommended in favor of Palantir-provided models, but the architecture reveals the underlying vector storage and KNN query mechanism.
Each Object Type participating in semantic search requires both a backing dataset with content and a vector property populated by an embedding pipeline.
Functions serve as the query interface for semantic search, wrapping KNN operations and making them accessible through the Ontology's standard function execution model.

title: Semantic search - Document processing - Palantir Docs

relevantParts: PDF and document chunking for semantic search
Unstructured content access through the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Document Chunking
extractedEntities.description: The process of breaking large text documents into smaller semantic chunks, each represented as a separate Ontology object linked back to the original document object for granular retrieval.

extractedEntities.name: Object Type
extractedEntities.description: Core Ontology entity used to represent both source documents and their derived chunks, with link relationships between parent document and child chunk objects.

extractedEntities.name: Link Type
extractedEntities.description: Ontology relationship connecting chunk objects back to their parent document objects, enabling navigation from search results to source documents.

extractedEntities.name: Property
extractedEntities.description: Named attributes on chunk objects including object_id, object_text, chunk_text, and chunk_index that store the segmented content and ordering metadata.

extractedEntities.description: Palantir's application builder used to surface semantic search results alongside rendered PDFs for source-of-truth cross-validation by users.

extractedEntities.description: A Pipeline Builder board that segments text into smaller pieces using configurable chunk size, separator priority, and overlap parameters.

extractedEntities.name: Media Set
extractedEntities.description: A Foundry data storage mechanism for importing and storing PDF documents and images before text extraction and chunking.

extractedEntities.name: Document-to-Chunk Pipeline
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where PDFs are imported as media sets, text is extracted, chunked into smaller segments, exploded into separate rows, and each chunk becomes its own Ontology object linked to the parent document.

extractedEntities.name: ObjectSet
extractedEntities.description: A filtered collection of chunk objects that results from a semantic search query, displayed alongside the original document for user validation.

keyInsights: Semantic search accuracy improves significantly when large documents are chunked into smaller pieces because embedding models have maximum input lengths and smaller text segments are more semantically distinct.
The chunking pipeline produces a one-to-many relationship: each source document Object maps to many chunk Objects via Link Types, preserving document provenance.

Pipeline Builder provides no-code chunking via the Chunk String board with configurable chunk size, separator priority hierarchy, and overlap parameters for maintaining context across chunk boundaries.
The recommended UX pattern surfaces search results alongside the rendered source PDF, enabling human cross-validation of AI-retrieved information.
Chunk objects carry metadata (chunk_index, parent object_id) that enables reassembly and ordering of results back into their original document context.

keyInsights: AIP's semantic search stack consists of seven sequential components: Virtual Tables for data connectivity, Embeddings for vectorization, Pipeline Builder for transformation, Ontology vector store for persistence, semantic search for querying, AIP Logic for orchestration, and AIP Assist for user interaction.
Virtual Tables eliminate data duplication by registering external cloud data assets (BigQuery, etc.) directly in AIP, enabling immediate building without ETL delays.
The Ontology serves as an enterprise vector store where embeddings are stored alongside structured data and governed by the same access controls as all other Ontology data.
AIP enables going from raw unstructured data to a production-grade semantic search application in minutes, not months, by composing existing platform capabilities.
Semantic similarity is demonstrated through embedding proximity: numerically alike embedding vectors indicate semantic similarity between concepts.

Access to LLM nodes requires explicit AIP capability permissions granted by platform administrators, maintaining security governance over AI feature usage.
LLM node output becomes standard dataset columns that can feed into subsequent Pipeline Builder transformations, enabling chaining of AI enrichment with traditional data processing.

The platform summary covers core architecture, terminology, and data integration patterns -- essentially a machine-readable Foundry primer that can be copy-pasted into AI agent conversations.

The summary was last updated December 19, 2025, and is maintained by Palantir engineers, establishing it as an authoritative reference for AI agent interactions with the platform.
This page represents Palantir's strategy of making the Ontology self-describing to AI systems, enabling AI agents to understand and operate within the platform without human intermediaries.

Support for on-premise, cloud-hosted, and fine-tuned models
