extractedEntities.name: Function
extractedEntities.description: Function on Objects capability enabling free-form code execution as part of actions within the Ontology

extractedEntities.name: Agent Isolation
extractedEntities.description: Design pattern where each AI agent is built for a specific purpose and configured to access specific views of the connected Ontology, navigating different paths through the object landscape at runtime

extractedEntities.name: Agentic Architecture
extractedEntities.description: Multi-agent architecture with classification agent, domain-specific agents (ETA, order management), master agent, and communication agent working together to process customer queries

extractedEntities.name: Human-in-the-Loop
extractedEntities.description: Pattern where human operators validate AI-suggested actions, provide feedback on incorrect suggestions, and handle escalated cases that require human judgment

extractedEntities.name: ObjectSet
extractedEntities.description: Specific views of the connected Ontology that agents are configured to access, representing filtered subsets of objects relevant to each agent's purpose

extractedEntities.name: Agent Data Navigation
extractedEntities.description: At runtime, agents navigate different paths through the object landscape to read information, following links between objects like Order to Customer to Shipping Information

extractedEntities.name: Customer Service Automation
extractedEntities.description: AI agents autonomously handle customer service queries including delivery ETA inquiries, order cancellations, and other customer experience processes with human validation

keyInsights: Actions are a first-class Ontology primitive that can drive in-platform changes (object edits), external changes (API calls), and free-form code execution (Function on Objects)
AI agents operate on specific views of the connected Ontology rather than having unrestricted access, implementing an agent isolation pattern for security and relevance

The multi-agent architecture includes specialized roles: classification agent for routing, domain-specific agents for processing, master agent for coordination, and communication agent for response drafting
Objects in the Ontology are hydrated through data pipelines from source systems like SAP, Salesforce, ERP systems, and data warehouses
Human-in-the-loop feedback is captured when agents suggest incorrect actions, enabling continuous improvement of the AI system
Agents navigate linked objects at runtime to gather information across the Ontology graph, such as following Order to Customer to Shipping Information paths

title: Understanding ROI with Palantir Foundry
summary: With Palantir Foundry, leaders can get a real-time view of their projects, and better measure ROI for more effective future strategy.

extractedEntities.name: Foundry
extractedEntities.description: Palantir's platform used internally and by customers for real-time financial visibility, integrating data from expense management, billing, payroll, revenue, and task-specific tools into a unified operational view.

extractedEntities.name: Data Integration
extractedEntities.description: Core pattern of connecting disparate financial databases (expense tools, billing management, Google Ads, AWS billing, payroll, revenue) into a unified data model for real-time ROI measurement.

extractedEntities.name: Ontology
extractedEntities.description: The unified semantic model that resolves impedance mismatches between different financial tools' data models, allowing organizational views that match actual business structure rather than being dictated by tool limitations.

extractedEntities.name: Real-Time ROI Measurement
extractedEntities.description: Use case where leaders get a real-time view of project investments and their performance, replacing weeks-long manual processes with instant data-driven decision-making.

extractedEntities.name: Financial Data Access Control
extractedEntities.description: Security challenge of ensuring accuracy and appropriate access to financial data, where mistakes can have market-moving implications, requiring careful technological controls for dissemination.

extractedEntities.name: Organisational Data Model Unification
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of creating a holistic data model that matches the actual organizational structure rather than being constrained by the fragmented views imposed by individual financial tools.

extractedEntities.name: Cross-System Data Reconciliation
extractedEntities.description: Technical challenge of marrying expenses to other metrics across different systems (travel, payroll, revenue) that have distinct data models and organizational vantage points.

extractedEntities.name: Decentralized Decision-Making
extractedEntities.description: Pattern of diffusing financial decision-making authority across the organization by providing appropriate real-time data access, replacing rigid top-down spending controls.

keyInsights: Financial ROI measurement is fundamentally a data integration problem: financial data is spread across databases that do not communicate, each with distinct data models and organizational vantage points.

Standard organizational views are fragmented by the tools used to manage them, forcing organizations to alter their structure to match tool limitations rather than business reality — the ontology resolves this impedance mismatch.
Financial data sensitivity creates a technological challenge where ensuring accuracy and appropriate access controls is critical, as mistakes can have market-moving implications.
Foundry enables a shift from rigid top-down spending controls to decentralized decision-making by providing real-time financial visibility to appropriate stakeholders.
Palantir uses Foundry internally for its own financial operations, demonstrating the platform's capability by dogfooding it for real-time ROI measurement across projects.

The Palantir Ontology enriches visual search detections by fusing them with additional data modalities and metadata, creating context about what, where, and when
The system combines pixel-based visual features with optional free-text prompts to leverage vision-language models for more specific detection
The approach addresses the critical military/intelligence need for real-time adaptation to new object types during active conflicts without waiting for traditional ML pipelines
